metadata.description: Preventive KB: Screening recommendations, vaccine schedules, and gap detection triggers. Source: USPSTF, CDC, CMS, ACIP.
sources: CMS Preventive Services (Medicare Benefit Policy Manual Ch.15 2024)
metadata.disclaimer: Screening recommendations are population-level guidance. Individual clinical decisions require physician judgment. Coverage details may vary by plan year and state.

target_population.high_risk: Earlier screening for: family history of CRC in first-degree relative <60 years, Lynch syndrome, familial adenomatous polyposis (FAP), personal history of polyps or IBD

recommended_tests.preferred_for: Most complete — therapeutic (polyp removal) at same time

recommended_tests.note: Non-invasive, no bowel prep. Positive requires colonoscopy follow-up.
Medicare covers every 3 years for age 50+. Higher false positive rate than FIT alone.

recommended_tests.frequency: Every 5 years (or every 10 years + annual FIT)

evidence.absolute_benefit: Colonoscopy: 3-4 fewer CRC deaths per 1000 screened over 25 years

gaps_detection.trigger: Member age 45-75 with no colonoscopy in past 10 years AND no FIT in past year AND no FIT-DNA in past 3 years
gaps_detection.action: Outreach for screening. Offer FIT as low-barrier first option.

insurance.medicare: Colonoscopy: covered every 10 years (every 2 years high risk) — no copay if no polyps found. FIT: covered annually. Cologuard: covered every 3 years age 50+. No copay for preventive colonoscopy (Note: If polyp removed during screening, some cost-sharing may apply — Bipartisan legislation eliminated this for Medicare 2023+).
insurance.uhc: Colonoscopy covered per USPSTF. FIT covered annually. Cologuard covered. Check specific plan for polyp removal cost-sharing.

source.document: USPSTF Colorectal Cancer Screening — Final Recommendation Statement

target_population.high_risk: Women with BRCA1/BRCA2 mutation or first-degree relative with mutation — earlier screening with MRI + mammography (USPSTF separate recommendation)

recommended_tests.test: Digital mammography or Digital Breast Tomosynthesis (3D mammography)
recommended_tests.frequency: Every 2 years for 40-74 (every year is an option — discussed with patient per shared decision-making)

evidence.mortality_reduction: Mammography reduces breast cancer mortality 15-20% in screened population
evidence.harms: False positives: 50-60% of women screened annually for 10 years will have a false positive requiring additional imaging. Overdiagnosis: 19% of screen-detected cancers may be overdiagnosed (DCIS).

entries.note_conflict: ACR/SBI recommend annual mammography starting at 40. USPSTF 2024 now recommends every-other-year starting at 40 (updated from 2016). ACS recommends annual at 45 with option at 40. See kb_conflicts.md CONFLICT_002.

gaps_detection.trigger: Female member age 40-74 with no mammogram in past 2 years
gaps_detection.action: Outreach for scheduling. Offer navigation support.

insurance.medicare: Annual screening mammography covered for women 40+ (once every 12 months). No copay. Diagnostic mammography (for symptoms) covered differently.
insurance.uhc: Annual mammography covered for women 40+. 3D mammography (tomosynthesis) covered.

source.document: USPSTF Breast Cancer Screening — Final Recommendation Statement 2024

target_population.not_indicated: Women <21, women >65 with adequate prior screening, women who have had a hysterectomy with cervix removal for non-cancer reasons

evidence.mortality_reduction: Routine cervical screening has reduced cervical cancer incidence and mortality >70% since 1970s
evidence.source: USPSTF Grade A 2018 (reaffirmed, HPV vaccine doesn't change screening recommendations)

gaps_detection.trigger: Female member age 21-65 with no Pap smear in past 3 years (or no co-test/hrHPV in past 5 years if age 30+)
gaps_detection.action: Outreach. Note: women who have received HPV vaccine still need cervical screening.

insurance.medicare: Pap smear and pelvic exam covered every 24 months (every 12 months for high risk). No copay.
insurance.uhc: Cervical screening covered per USPSTF intervals. No copay as preventive.

source.document: USPSTF Cervical Cancer Screening — Final Recommendation Statement

target_population.general: Adults 50-80 years who have a 20 pack-year smoking history AND currently smoke or quit within past 15 years
target_population.discontinue: Once not smoked for 15+ years or health condition limiting life expectancy or ability to undergo curative surgery

recommended_tests.test: Annual low-dose CT (LDCT) scan of the chest

evidence.mortality_reduction: NLST trial: 20% reduction in lung cancer mortality. NELSON trial: 24% reduction in men, 33% in women.
evidence.absolute_benefit: ~3 fewer lung cancer deaths per 1000 screened over 6 years
evidence.harms: False positives: ~25% have positive screens; most are benign. Radiation exposure (low). Incidental findings requiring follow-up.

gaps_detection.trigger: Member age 50-80 with documented 20+ pack-year smoking history, current smoker or quit <15 years, no LDCT in past year
gaps_detection.action: Outreach with smoking cessation counseling offer AND LDCT scheduling.

insurance.medicare: Annual LDCT covered for ages 50-77 meeting criteria. Must include counseling on benefits/harms and smoking cessation intervention.
insurance.uhc: Covered per USPSTF criteria. Prior auth may be required.

source.document: USPSTF Lung Cancer Screening — Final Recommendation Statement

target_population.general: Adults 35-70 years who are overweight (BMI ≥25) or obese (BMI ≥30)
target_population.high_risk: Screen earlier for: gestational diabetes history, polycystic ovary syndrome, family history T2DM, Hispanic/Latino, Black, Asian, Native American, Pacific Islander populations (at lower BMI cutoffs — AAPI BMI ≥23)

recommended_tests.frequency: Every 3 years if normal; annually if prediabetic
Every 3 years if normal; annually if prediabetic

prediabetes_intervention.intervention: CDC-recognized Diabetes Prevention Program (DPP) — 5-7% weight loss + 150 min/week exercise = 58% reduction in T2DM development (DPP trial NEJM 2002)

gaps_detection.trigger: Member age 35-70 with BMI ≥25 and no HbA1c or fasting glucose in past 3 years
gaps_detection.action: Outreach for lab work. If prediabetic, enroll in DPP program.

insurance.medicare: Diabetes screening covered for high-risk adults (up to 2 tests/year if high risk). DPP program covered under Part B for prediabetes. No copay for preventive screening.
insurance.uhc: Diabetes screening covered. DPP program covered for eligible members.

source.document: USPSTF Screening for Prediabetes and Type 2 Diabetes — Final Recommendation Statement

recommended_tests.frequency: Adults 18-39 with normal BP (<130/85) and no risk factors: every 3-5 years. Adults 40+ or elevated risk: annually.

evidence.benefit: Detecting and treating HTN reduces stroke by 35-40%, MI by 20-25%, heart failure by 50%

gaps_detection.trigger: Member age 40+ with no BP reading documented in past 12 months
gaps_detection.action: Remind at next healthcare visit. Many pharmacies offer free BP checks.

insurance.medicare: BP screening covered at Annual Wellness Visit and Welcome to Medicare Visit. No copay.
insurance.uhc: BP screening covered as preventive service. No copay.

source.document: USPSTF Hypertension Screening — Final Recommendation Statement

entries.uspstf_grade: B (for statin use in prevention), no grade for screening alone

target_population.general: Adults 21+ with cardiovascular risk factors. ACC/AHA recommends universal screening.
target_population.high_priority: Adults 40-75 years for ASCVD risk assessment and statin decision-making

recommended_tests.test: Fasting lipid panel (Total cholesterol, LDL, HDL, triglycerides)
recommended_tests.frequency: Every 5 years if normal and low risk. Every 1-2 years if on treatment or elevated risk. Annually for high-risk (diabetes, CAD, CKD).

statin_preventive_use.uspstf_recommendation: Grade B — Low to moderate dose statin for adults 40-75 with no CVD history, 1+ CVD risk factors, and 10-year ASCVD risk ≥10% (pooled cohort equations)
statin_preventive_use.source: USPSTF 2022 Statin Use for Prevention of CVD

gaps_detection.trigger: Member age 40+ with diabetes or hypertension and no lipid panel in past 12 months
gaps_detection.action: Order fasting lipid panel at next visit.

insurance.medicare: Cardiovascular disease screening blood tests (total cholesterol, LDL, HDL, TG) covered once every 5 years. More frequently when monitoring treatment under Part B (medical necessity).
insurance.uhc: Lipid panels covered for preventive screening and treatment monitoring.

source.document: ACC/AHA 2019 Cardiovascular Risk Guideline + USPSTF Statin Use 2022

target_population.women: Women 65 years and older. Postmenopausal women <65 years whose 10-year fracture risk (FRAX) is ≥9.3% (equal to 65-year-old white woman with no other risk factors).
target_population.men: USPSTF: Insufficient evidence (I statement). NOF: Screen men 70+. ACP: Screen men 65-70+ with risk factors.

recommended_tests.frequency: Every 2 years while on treatment; every 2-5 years for monitoring in those not on treatment

gaps_detection.trigger: Female member age 65+ with no DXA in past 2 years OR postmenopausal female <65 with documented risk factors and no DXA
gaps_detection.action: Refer for DXA. Calculate FRAX score.

insurance.medicare: DXA covered every 24 months for: women 65+, postmenopausal women with fracture risk factors, men 65+ on Medicare (non-USPSTF coverage), patients on long-term glucocorticoids. No copay for preventive indication.
insurance.uhc: DXA covered per USPSTF for women 65+. Additional coverage for high-risk postmenopausal women.

source.document: USPSTF Osteoporosis to Prevent Fractures — Final Recommendation Statement

entries.target_population: All adults 18 years and older. Including pregnant and postpartum women (Grade B).

recommended_tests.note: If positive, administer PHQ-9 for severity

recommended_tests.frequency: When PHQ-2 positive or depression suspected

gaps_detection.trigger: Member with no depression screening in past 12 months at preventive visit
gaps_detection.action: Include PHQ-2 in annual wellness visit. Address positive screens.

insurance.medicare: Depression screening covered once per year at Annual Wellness Visit. No copay.
insurance.uhc: Depression screening covered as preventive service.

source.document: USPSTF Depression, Anxiety, and Suicide Risk in Adults — Final Recommendation Statement

target_population.criteria: Men 65-75 years who have EVER smoked (≥100 cigarettes in lifetime)
target_population.not_recommended: Women who have never smoked (Grade D). Women who have ever smoked (insufficient evidence — I statement).

recommended_tests.frequency: One-time screen. Surveillance if small AAA found (3.0-5.4cm every 6-12 months).

evidence.mortality_reduction: AAA rupture has 80% mortality. Elective repair <2% mortality. Screening reduces AAA-related mortality 43% in men.

gaps_detection.trigger: Male member age 65-75 with smoking history and no AAA ultrasound on record
gaps_detection.action: One-time ultrasound screening. Coordinate with PCP.

insurance.medicare: One-time AAA ultrasound covered for qualifying male smokers 65-75 at Welcome to Medicare visit. No copay.

source.document: USPSTF Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Screening — Final Recommendation Statement

target_population.general: All adults and adolescents 15-65 years (at least once)
target_population.high_risk: Annual screening for: MSM, injection drug users, multiple sex partners, sex with HIV+ person

recommended_tests.frequency: At least once for all 15-65. Annually for high-risk.

gaps_detection.trigger: Member age 15-65 with no HIV test on record
gaps_detection.action: Offer HIV testing at next healthcare visit.

insurance.medicare: HIV screening covered annually for age 15-65 and for high-risk older adults. No copay.
insurance.uhc: HIV screening covered as preventive service.

source.document: USPSTF HIV Infection Screening — Final Recommendation Statement

intervention.threshold: BMI ≥30: Offer or refer to intensive, multicomponent behavioral intervention
intervention.program: Intensive Behavioral Therapy (IBT): 12-26 sessions in first year. Covered by Medicare with no copay.
intervention.content: Goal-setting, self-monitoring, problem-solving, behavioral strategies for diet and physical activity

gaps_detection.trigger: Member with BMI ≥30 and no behavioral counseling or weight management program engagement in past 12 months
gaps_detection.action: Refer to IBT program. Coordinate with PCP for pharmacotherapy evaluation.

insurance.medicare: IBT for obesity: covered 22 sessions in 12 months (first 3 months: weekly; months 4-6: biweekly; months 7-12: monthly if 3kg lost). No copay. Must be delivered by primary care provider.
insurance.uhc: Weight management programs covered. Commercial plans vary widely on AOM (anti-obesity medication) coverage.

source.document: USPSTF Weight Loss to Prevent Obesity-Related Morbidity and Mortality in Adults — Final Recommendation Statement

entries.screening_type: Statin Use for Primary CVD Prevention
entries.target_population: Adults 40-75 years with 1+ CVD risk factors (dyslipidemia, diabetes, hypertension, or smoking) AND estimated 10-year CVD event risk ≥10%

recommended_intervention.test: Calculate 10-year ASCVD risk (ACC/AHA Pooled Cohort Equations)

recommended_intervention.action: Prescribe low-to-moderate dose statin if criteria met

evidence.benefit: Statins reduce major cardiovascular events (MI, stroke, cardiovascular death) by ~25% in primary prevention for qualifying patients

gaps_detection.trigger: Member age 40-75 with diabetes or hypertension or hyperlipidemia, calculated 10-year ASCVD risk ≥10%, and no statin on medication list
gaps_detection.action: Flag for PCP review. Discuss statin initiation at next visit.

insurance.medicare_part_d: Generic statins (atorvastatin, simvastatin, pravastatin, rosuvastatin) Tier 1 on most formularies. <$5/month generic cost.
insurance.uhc: Generic statins Tier 1. No prior auth required for generics.

source.document: USPSTF Statin Use for the Primary Prevention of CVD Events — Final Recommendation Statement

entries.target_population: All persons ≥6 months without contraindications — annually

recommended_vaccine.high_dose_preferred: Adults 65+: High-dose IIV4 (Fluzone HD) or adjuvanted IIV4 (Fluad) — 24% more effective than standard dose in older adults
recommended_vaccine.frequency: Annually, preferably by end of October. Still beneficial if given later.

evidence.benefit: Reduces influenza-related hospitalizations 40-60% in general population. High-dose IIV4 reduces hospitalizations 26% more than standard dose in adults 65+.
evidence.source: ACIP 2024 Adult Immunization Schedule + DiazGranados NEJM 2014

gaps_detection.trigger: Member with no influenza vaccination in current flu season (September–April)
gaps_detection.action: Remind members each October. Offer pharmacy-administered vaccine information.

insurance.medicare: Part B covers annual flu shot at no cost. No deductible or copay.
insurance.uhc: Annual flu shot covered as preventive. Available at in-network pharmacies and providers.

target_population.adults_65_plus: PCV20 (single dose, preferred) OR PCV15 followed by PPSV23 (8 weeks later) if not previously vaccinated
target_population.adults_19_64_high_risk: PCV (any above) for immunocompromising conditions, CSF leak, cochlear implant, chronic lung/heart/liver/kidney disease, diabetes, alcoholism, smoking

evidence.benefit: Pneumococcal disease causes ~150,000 hospitalizations in adults/year in US. PCV20 covers 77% of pneumococcal strains causing invasive disease in adults.

gaps_detection.trigger: Member age 65+ with no pneumococcal vaccine on record
gaps_detection.action: Offer PCV20 at next clinical encounter.

insurance.medicare: Part B covers pneumococcal vaccines with no copay. PCV15 + PPSV23 or PCV20 both covered.

source.document: ACIP Pneumococcal Vaccination Recommendations for Adults (2021, updated 2024)

target_population.immunocompetent: Adults 50 years and older: 2 doses of RZV (Shingrix), 2-6 months apart
target_population.immunocompromised: Adults 19+ who are or will be immunodeficient (2 doses, 1-2 months apart)

entries.vaccine: Recombinant Zoster Vaccine (RZV/Shingrix) — preferred over older live-attenuated ZVL (Zostavax, discontinued in US 2020)

evidence.efficacy: Shingrix 97.2% efficacy against shingles in adults 50-69; 91.3% in adults 70+. Efficacy persists 10+ years.
evidence.postherpetic_neuralgia: Reduces PHN (chronic nerve pain post-shingles) by >90%

entries.note: Prior chickenpox disease or prior Zostavax vaccination — still recommend Shingrix.

gaps_detection.trigger: Member age 50+ with fewer than 2 doses of Shingrix (RZV) on record
gaps_detection.action: Recommend Shingrix 2-dose series. Available at pharmacies.

insurance.medicare: Shingrix covered under Part D (NOT Part B) — coverage and copay vary by plan. Some gap/low copay plans cover at low/no cost.
insurance.uhc: Covered under pharmacy benefit (Part D). Check specific plan formulary.
insurance.note: Unlike flu and pneumococcal (Part B — $0 copay), Shingrix under Part D may have significant copay depending on plan. IRMAA and coverage gap may apply.

source.document: ACIP Recommendations for Use of Herpes Zoster Vaccines (2018, updated 2022)

target_population.priority_boosters: Adults 65+ and immunocompromised: additional dose of 2024-2025 updated mRNA vaccine recommended

recommended_vaccine.2024_2025_season: Updated mRNA vaccines targeting current circulating variants (Moderna or Pfizer-BioNTech). ACIP recommends all adults ≥65 receive additional dose of 2024-2025 vaccine.

gaps_detection.trigger: Member 65+ with no updated 2024-2025 COVID-19 vaccine
gaps_detection.action: Strongly recommend for all adults 65+. Note: recommendations evolve — check CDC.gov for current guidance.

insurance.medicare: COVID-19 vaccines covered under Part B at no cost.

entries.note: COVID-19 vaccine recommendations evolve rapidly with emerging variants and evidence. Always verify against current CDC/ACIP guidance at cdc.gov/vaccines.

entries.screening_type: Tobacco Use Screening and Cessation Intervention

intervention.brief_counseling: 5 A's: Ask, Advise, Assess, Assist, Arrange. Even brief (3 min) physician advice increases quit rate.

most_effective_pharmacotherapy.efficacy: OR 5.1x vs. no treatment; most effective single agent. Watch for neuropsychiatric symptoms.
OR 2.4x vs. placebo; useful if comorbid depression

gaps_detection.trigger: Member with documented current smoker status and no cessation counseling or medication in past 12 months
gaps_detection.action: Brief counseling at every visit. Offer varenicline prescription or NRT. 1-800-QUIT-NOW (national quitline).

insurance.medicare: Tobacco cessation counseling covered Part B — 8 sessions/year. No copay. Varenicline covered Part D (generic available). NRT covered Part D.
insurance.uhc: Cessation counseling covered. Varenicline and NRT covered Part D. Quitline referral covered.

source.document: USPSTF Tobacco Smoking Cessation in Adults, Including Pregnant Persons — Final Recommendation Statement
